chapter: Chapter: Nature of Matter – Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

questions.question: What is matter?
questions.answer: Anything that has mass and occupies space.

questions.question: What is a mixture?
questions.answer: A combination of two or more substances not chemically combined.

questions.question: What are the components of a mixture?
questions.answer: The individual substances present in a mixture.

questions.question: What is a uniform mixture?
questions.answer: A mixture in which components are evenly distributed.

questions.question: Give one example of a non-uniform mixture.
questions.answer: Sand mixed with water.

questions.question: Why is air considered a mixture?
questions.answer: Because it contains different gases mixed together.

questions.question: What happens when carbon dioxide is passed through limewater?
questions.answer: Limewater turns milky.

questions.question: What is a pure substance?
questions.answer: A substance made of only one type of particle.

questions.question: What is an element?
questions.answer: A pure substance made of only one type of atom.

questions.question: What is an atom?
questions.answer: The smallest particle of an element that retains its properties.

questions.question: What is a molecule?
questions.answer: A group of two or more atoms chemically bonded together.

questions.question: What is electrolysis?
questions.answer: The process of breaking a compound using electricity.

questions.question: Which gases are produced during electrolysis of water?
questions.answer: Hydrogen and oxygen.

questions.question: What is a compound?
questions.answer: A substance formed by chemical combination of two or more elements.

questions.question: Give one example of a compound.
questions.answer: Water.

questions.question: What happens when iron and sulfur are heated together?
questions.answer: They form iron sulfide.

questions.question: What is an alloy?
questions.answer: A mixture of two or more metals.

questions.question: Give one example of an alloy.
questions.answer: Bronze.

questions.question: What is a mineral?
questions.answer: A naturally occurring solid substance found in the Earth.

questions.question: Give one example of a mineral.
questions.answer: Quartz.

questions.question: How can mixtures be separated?
questions.answer: By physical methods.

questions.question: Which method separates sand from water?
questions.answer: Filtration.

questions.question: Which method separates dissolved salt from water?
questions.answer: Evaporation.

questions.question: Which method separates iron filings from sand?
questions.answer: Magnetic separation.

questions.question: What is the main classification of matter in this chapter?
questions.answer: Elements, compounds, and mixtures.
